content:
Phase 5 Cycle 1 (5.1 worker reliability + 5.2 security) implemented — commit 081680f. This impl review covers the two blocker slices; 5.3/5.4/5.5 remain as later impl cycles per the approved plan's ordering.

5.1 worker reliability:
- Retry gate added to both eligibility queries (db.py): localization_eligible_reports enforces max_localize_retries=3; reports_eligible_for_fix enforces max_fix_retries=2. Exhaustion is DERIVED from failed analysis rows (_retry_blocked / _failed_attempts_after), scoped to failures since the last success (localization) / current localization (fix), plus retry_delay_seconds. Reports stay 'triaged' and are simply not re-dispatched — no new status, no deferred overload (as you resolved in plan round 1).
- Stage 3 already wrote failed rows; Stage 4 (propose_fix) now records a failed 'fix' row on real failures and classifies transient vs permanent via _classify_llm_error (auth/bad-request -> permanent/no-retry; timeout/429/5xx -> transient). Precondition misses (no/stale localization, no candidates) raise FixProposalDefer and reset to triaged WITHOUT recording a failure, so they never count toward the paid-call cap.
- report_failure_info surfaces failed_stage/last_error in GET /reports/{id} and the queue overview (new QueueOverview.failed list). POST /api/v1/queue/{id}/retry now clears failed triage/localization/fix rows via reset_stage_retries (previously triage-only).
- Real /health: DB + Ollama (GET /api/tags, 2s timeout) + worker-alive; 503 only when DB unreachable, else 'degraded'. Added dependency-free /health/live for the supervisor. httpx promoted to a direct runtime dep.
- Optional Ollama cooldown deliberately deferred (documented in §5.1) — not needed for the blocker.

5.2 security:
- CORS closed by default (middleware only mounted when BUGALIZER_CORS_ORIGINS set); removed allow_origins=['*'].
- Startup warning logged when BUGALIZER_API_KEYS is empty (auth disabled).
- Fernet stub removed (settings.secret_key + projects.api_key_encrypted schema column); decision_log.md updated to CONFIRMED/implemented per your plan-review note. Old DBs keep the unused column harmlessly.
- /security-check run: clean for this diff; it resolved the prior HIGH findings (CORS *, auth-disabled-no-warning). Remaining items are pre-existing and low (git URL SSRF already blocked to https/git@; loose dep pins intentional).

Tests: 153 passing (+11). New coverage — localization retry cap + delay + reset; fix retry cap (transient) + permanent-no-retry + reset; report_failure_info; transient/permanent failure recording and defer-no-record in propose_fix; _classify_llm_error; GET /reports failed fields; queue overview failed list; CORS closed-by-default + configured-origin; /health liveness + readiness checks. Plan doc §5.1/§5.2 checkboxes updated.

Approved. Reviewed Phase 5 Cycle 1 implementation for 5.1 worker reliability and 5.2 security. The retry gates are derived from failed analysis rows as approved: localization_eligible_reports uses failed localization rows since the last success; reports_eligible_for_fix uses failed fix rows since the current localization and honors permanent failures plus max_fix_retries/retry_delay_seconds. propose_fix now records failed fix rows for real failures, classifies transient vs permanent errors, and defers precondition misses without counting them toward paid-call retry caps. Failure surfacing is wired into GET /reports/{id} and queue overview, and /queue/{id}/retry clears triage/localization/fix failed rows. Security changes close CORS by default, add the auth-disabled startup warning, and remove the Fernet stub with the decision log updated. Targeted API/queue/fix-proposer tests passed: 75 passed. Full suite passed: 153 passed. Non-blocking note: no-candidate/precondition defers can still cause worker churn, but they do not spend cloud calls and are outside the blocker acceptance.
